name: Storm-Lancers

recruitment.method: No nets. No violence. Use Beetle-Bait and Throat-Clicking (mimicry) to coax the frightened animal.

gear.saddle: Carved from light pumice stone and lined with leather. Provides a stable platform for the heavy Scute physique.
gear.grounding_chain: A heavy copper chain drags from the saddle to ground any residual static charge when landing.

weaponry.ram: The Ray uses its bone crest to smash through the wooden hulls of enemy airships.
weaponry.stinger-whip: The rider guides the Ray to 'tail-whip' enemy engines, discharging a massive chaos-shock that disables Aether-Tech.
weaponry.ray-daggers: Warriors carry harvested stingers in stone sheaths as single-use biological grenades.

status_symbol: A Scute General riding a War-Ray is the ultimate projection of power. It says, 'I conquered the sky with my bare hands.'

biology.reptile_connection: The Scute (Lizards/Crocodiles) and the Rays share a distant evolutionary ancestor.
biology.communication_interface: The Rays use their chameleon skin [User Input] to signal emotion. The Scute, being observant reptiles, can read these subtle color shifts instinctively.

stinger.biology: The Ray absorbs ambient Chaos radiation. Its liver processes this radiation, transmuting the entropy into a volatile biological fluid stored in the tail sac.

mechanism.on_the_beast: When the Ray stings, it injects the fluid. The fluid reacts violently with the target's blood or engine fuel, causing rapid necrosis or combustion.
mechanism.trophy: A harvested stinger is effectively a sealed glass dagger filled with acid.

gear.saddle: The saddles are carved from light, pumice-like stone and lined with leather, grounding the rider and providing a stable platform for the heavy Scute physique.

phase_1.lure: The Scute climber spends days on the peak. They don't hide; they project 'calm dominance.'
phase_1.bait: They use crushed Gem-Shell Beetles (the Ray's favorite snack) or vibrate the rock to mimic a beetle swarm.
phase_1.vocalization: They utilize a 'Throat-Rattle'—a sound that mimics a Ray's mating or nesting hum to lower its guard.
